title: Snr Applied Scientist
description:
Overview



   Copilot Discover helps hundreds of millions of people be informed, entertained, and inspired by surfacing highly relevant, trustworthy, and delightful content across Microsoft surfaces. We’re building the next generation of AI‑powered quality understanding and recommendation systems—spanning text, images, audio, and video—to curate the right content at the right moment while upholding safety and integrity.


As a
 Senior Applied Scientist
, you’ll lead the science behind Discover’s ranking and content‑quality stack, combining LLMs, multimodal models, and large‑scale recommender systems to drive measurable gains in engagement, satisfaction, and trust. You will set technical direction, mentor a high‑caliber science cohort, and partner closely with engineering, PM, UXR, and policy to ship end‑to‑end outcomes. You will contribute to the development of the next generation of MSN that is adopting the latest generative AI techniques.


Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.


Starting January 26, 2026, Microsoft AI (MAI) employees who live within a 50- mile commute of a designated Microsoft office in the U.S. or 25-mile commute of a non-U.S., country-specific location are expected to work from the office at least four days per week. This expectation is subject to local law and may vary by jurisdiction.

   Responsibilities






Lead content‑quality understanding at scale. Design and deploy models that assess credibility, usefulness, freshness, safety, and diversity across modalities; reduce misinformation/toxicity error rates through prompt‑ and model‑level innovations; build human‑in‑the‑loop and active‑learning pipelines that get better over time.


Champion safety & trust. Partner with policy and platform teams to encode safety standards and editorial principles into the ML system; create red‑teaming, adversarial, and safeguard layers for generative and curated experiences.


Scale E2E ML systems. Collaborate with engineering on data contracts, feature stores, distributed training/inference, and automated rollout/rollback; drive architectural investments that increase agility and reliability of Discover’s AI platform.


Own evaluation and experimentation. Define offline metrics (e.g., Rejection Rate, ERR, Defect Rate) and online methodologies (A/B tests, interleaving, counterfactual & bandit approaches) to confidently attribute business impact and guard against regressions.


Mentor & influence. Provide technical leadership across problem framing, methodology selection, code quality, and publishing/knowledge‑sharing; uplevel peers through design reviews, deep‑dives, and principled decision‑


Stay close to users. Translate user engagements and behavioral history into model objectives and product bets; ensure our AI solutions elevate relevance, transparency, and engagement for real users.




 Qualifications



    Required Qualifications:

title: Semiconductors Engineer
description:
Header






At Capgemini, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous vehicles, Electric vehicles to semiconductors, and life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.










Job Description






We are looking for an RF-focused Analog Layout Engineer with expertise in FinFET nodes. The candidate will design layouts for high-frequency analog/RF blocks, ensuring optimal performance under stringent parasitic and matching constraints.


Key Responsibilities




Layout design for RF circuits such as LNAs, mixers, VCOs, and PLL sub-blocks in FinFET technology.


Optimize routing for high-frequency signals, minimizing parasitic capacitance and inductance.


Implement shielding strategies (coaxial, differential, ground shields) to reduce coupling and crosstalk.


Address EM/IR reliability for high-current RF paths and ensure compliance with advanced DRC/LVS rules.


Apply matching techniques for RF differential pairs and passive components (inductors, capacitors).


Collaborate with RF designers to meet performance targets for gain, noise figure, and phase noise.


Ensure compliance with color-aware routing and multi-patterning constraints for RF metal layers.






Required Skills and Competencies:


Core Technical Skills:




Hands-on experience in RF layout design at FinFET nodes.


Deep understanding of parasitic control for high-frequency circuits.


Familiarity with EM modeling, substrate coupling, and isolation techniques.


Proficiency in RF-aware EDA tools and post-layout simulation flows.


Knowledge of reliability and aging effects in RF devices and mitigation strategies.




Preferred Skills




Experience with RF sign-off flows and EM extraction tools.


Ability to optimize layout for low phase noise and high linearity.


Scripting skills for RF layout automation and verification.






Soft Skills & Competencies:




Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.






Excellent communication and documentation abilities.


Leadership skills to guide development teams and drive technical decisions.


Collaboration & Stakeholder Management


Time Management & Attentiveness


Learning Mindset


Verbal & Written Communication






Preferred Qualifications:




Bachelor’s or master’s degree in electrical engineering, Computer Engineering, or related field.


3-6 years of relevant experience.












Footer






Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: AI Architect
description:
Join our Team

Being part of Ericsson empowers you to learn, lead, and perform at your best, shaping future technology. Within the Solution Area Cognitive Networks Solutions Software R&D (SA CNS SW R&D), we offer the opportunity to collaborate with highly qualified Global teams and to enable success stories for our customers. Proven expertise in LLM architectures (e.g., Transformers, GANs, VAEs), fine-tuning, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Context embedding, vector databases, and semantic search tools.


Strong coding skills in Python for robust AI development and packaging.

Deep understanding and hands-on experience in building Microservices based applications following the API First approach for serving ML models.


Proficiency with loading data from different data repositories: SQL Databases, Data Warehouses, and Data Lakes.

Excellent stakeholder management, cross-functional collaboration, and leadership skills (including coaching, facilitation, and program delivery).

Knowledge of Agile Software Development Methodologies.

title: Marketing Specialist (6-Month Contract), Emerging Stores Books
description:
DESCRIPTION



  This job role is a seasonal role (FTC- Fixed term contract) for 6 months contract on the payroll of Amazon. 
  



   At Amazon, we are working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. 
  

   We are seeking a self-starter who can quickly familiarize themselves with this fast-paced environment, build relationships/trust with cross-functional teams, and assume ownership within the program. You must be able to thrive and succeed in an entrepreneurial environment, and not be hindered by ambiguity or competing priorities. High judgment, analytical talent and good and concise communication skills are essential to success in this role. 
  



   We are looking for a smart, results-oriented, to be a part of growing our business. The successful candidate will be responsible for planning and coordinate execution of best in class customer engagement strategies. 
  



   As an Marketing Specialist you will ensure that customer experience and expectations are planned, set and meet a high standard. You will coordinate the execution of traffic drivers (emails/push, home pages campaigns, category pages, and event pages) based on the instructions/inputs agreed with Product Management team and key stakeholders. Also, you’ll own the preparation of report on performance and traffic data to our management teams to influence CX and traffic roadmaps. You will be involved in coordinating with multiple stakeholders across the business: from category managers, Marketing Managers, designers and content creators from multiple different marketplaces. 
  



   Key job responsibilities 
  

   Amazon retail teams work to help improve the selection and shopping experience for customers across our product categories, from books to fashion, electronics to grocery, movies and music to toys and health & beauty, and everything in between. As a retail intern in one of those categories, you will learn how Amazon handles its e-commerce retail business. Working alongside a vendor manager to help provide a suite of services designed to support Amazon's most strategic brands within one of our retail categories. Your priority will be to work backwards from the customer, identifying inputs to grow brands and improve their performance. 
  

   Amazon advertising sits at the intersection of advertising and e-commerce. Customers visit us every day to find, discover, and buy products, so we’re obsessed with making the shopping experience the best it can be – and advertising is a part of that experience. As an intern, you’ll gain a deep understanding of Amazon's display advertising products as well as our technical and operational capabilities. You’ll learn when and how we can create custom opportunities, and you’ll help our account managers create compelling presentations to delight our clients. 
  

   Amazon merchant services covers all third party merchants’ activity related to selling on Amazon as well as the specific services and products we offer to help develop and grow these sellers. 
  



   Main responsibilities will include: 
  




Driving end-to-end merchandising roadmaps (incl. planning and coordinate its execution), continuously monitoring outputs, prioritizing based on impact, and delivering against plans. This includes email programs, new product launches, category placements, developing and executing Amazon site-wide and Books specific events WW.


Working on marketing strategies to drive demand and new customer acquisition across multiple online traffic channels.


Working closely with multiple stakeholders such as product managers, designers, data scientists, and other international partner teams.


Demonstrating excellent analytical and technical abilities, and use data to make recommendations that will improve customers’ shopping experiences, and also drive key business decisions.


Operating autonomously, delivering against merchandising plans.


Developing and execute ideas for innovation, automation, and efficiency.


Becoming an expert for Amazon online marketing tools, processes, and related performance metrics.





   A day in the life 
  

   As a marketing specialist, you will be matched to a mentor. 
  

   In addition to learning about an impactful project, you will have the 
  

   opportunity to engage with Amazonians for both personal and professional 
  

   development, expand your network, and participate in activities with other 
  

   interns throughout your internship. No matter the location of your internship, 
  

   we give you the tools to own your project and learn in a real-world setting.
 


 BASIC QUALIFICATIONS




1+ years of account management, project or program management or buying experience


Experience using Excel and other business analytic tools




 PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS




Experience with software and editing tools (including HTML, Excel and SQL)


Experience in online retail


Experience using analytical specific tools such as Google Analytics, SQL or HTML


Bachelor's degree





  Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Drive the integration of models into production-ready pipelines, establishing and managing version control, validation, and automated testing strategies.




2. Technical Strategy & Leadership 






Problem Framing: Frame use cases with business teams, validate data availability, and use rigorous analysis to break down ambiguous business questions into testable hypotheses and concrete data science projects. Define success metrics (KPIs) upfront to ensure every project has a measurable business impact.




Technology Assessment: Lead "make-or-buy" decisions by evaluating the trade-offs between building proprietary models versus integrating third-party AI services, always aligning with business objectives for speed, cost, and long-term value.




Mentorship & Standardization: 
Act as a technical reference, providing leadership and mentorship to junior data scientists through code reviews and knowledge sharing. Drive technical excellence by standardizing frameworks and improving our technical stack, modeling approaches, and deployment practices.




Innovation: 
S tay ahead of market trends and conduct active research on the latest advancements in AI/ML to identify and propose innovative approaches and new use cases. This includes attending conferences,, and participating in meetups and internal learnshops.




3. Collaboration & Influence 






Data-Driven Collaboration: Act as the analytical authority within cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering, Business). Challenge existing assumptions with data, guide strategic decisions with analytical evidence, and ensure model outputs are directly integrated into business workflows.




Insightful Communication: Go beyond just presenting results. Clearly explain the "why" behind your model's logic, its limitations (e.g., biases, confidence levels), and the trade-offs between different analytical approaches to both technical and executive audiences.




Actionable Visualization: C reate clear dashboards and visualizations that tell a story with the data, empowering stakeholders to make data-informed decisions independently.








Cross-Functional Leadership: 
Lead collaboration with IT and Data Engineering teams to enhance data accessibility, harmonization, pipeline robustness, and streamline model deployment.




Data Strategy: 
Evaluate data availability, coverage, and quality, proposing pragmatic approaches for calibration, substitution, or enrichment when datasets are incomplete or imperfect.






Professional 
Experience 


We are looking for a candidate who embodies three core strengths:


1. Technical & Execution Excellence 
You are a master builder who ships robust, production-grade solutions. 






After a master degree and PhD , you bring 5+ years of applied experience as a Data Scientist, with a proven track record of taking complex Machine Learning projects from initial POC to full production deployment.




Your experience comes from a fast-paced, commercial environment (such as E-commerce, FinTech, Online Travel, SaaS, or modern Tech Consulting), where you have successfully built and shipped models for dynamic pricing, demand forecasting, or resource optimization.




Your technical toolkit is extensive, with an excellent mastery of Python and its core data science libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost, pandas, NumPy).




You have strong knowledge of the full MLOps and Data Engineering lifecycle, including SQL, containerization (Docker), workflow orchestration (Airflow), CI/CD, and model deployment/serving (MLflow, Kubernetes, REST APIs).




You have a deep understanding of Generative AI architectures and workflows (embeddings, RAG, fine-tuning) and have experience with GenAI/LLM projects.




You are adept at creating clear, insightful visualizations using tools like Looker, Tableau, or Power BI and applying Agile/scrum methodologies to deliver results iteratively.




You have hands-on experience working with large-scale datasets in cloud environments (e.g., AWS, GCP).




2. Strategic & Business Acumen 
You think like a business owner and translate complex data into tangible value. 






You have a proven ability to deconstruct ambiguous business problems into a clear analytical framework, defining testable hypotheses and the KPIs that will measure success.




You demonstrate strong project management skills, taking ownership of the entire project lifecycle to ensure solutions are delivered on time and create measurable business impact.




You practice statistical rigor, going beyond model accuracy to validate choices, quantify uncertainty, and ensure the reliability and fairness of your results in a real-world context.




You have led root-cause investigations, using data to diagnose why business metrics deviate from expectations and recommending a clear solution.




You demonstrate exceptional business impact translation, turning complex model outputs into a compelling narrative that influences leadership-level decisions.




3. Leadership & Collaborative Influence 
You are a natural coach and communicator who elevates the entire team. 






You possess strong leadership and mentoring skills with a genuine passion for sharing knowledge, helping others grow, and fostering a collaborative team environment.




Your communication skills are excellent, giving you the ability to tell a compelling story with data and distill complex topics into simple, clear explanations for any audience.




You have a proactive, autonomous, and results-oriented mindset. You are intellectually curious, pragmatic, and thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative setting.




You have the business acumen to understand complex challenges and translate them into adapted technical solutions.

title: ETIC, Data Architect - Senior Manager
description:

Job Description & Summary 
At PwC, our people in data management focus on organising and maintaining data to enable accuracy and accessibility for effective decision-making. These individuals handle data governance, quality control, and data integration to support business operations. 
 



  In data architecture at PwC, you will focus on designing and implementing the structure and organisation of data assets within the organisation. Your work will involve developing data models, defining data standards, and establishing data integration frameworks. You will assist in confirming that data is stored, accessed, and utilised efficiently, enabling seamless data flow across systems and supporting effective data management practices. 
 




PwC ETIC is seeking a seasoned Data Architect with extensive experience in designing and implementing enterprise-level data platforms. The ideal candidate will have over 10 years of experience in data architecture, with a focus on working with hyperscale platforms. You will be responsible for defining, creating, and overseeing scalable data architectures that enable organizations to leverage data for strategic business initiatives. A strong background in working with cloud hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) is preferred


Key Responsibilities:


Data Architecture Design:






Lead the design and development of scalable and flexible data architectures to meet complex business requirements across various platforms and environments.




Create comprehensive data models, data flow diagrams, and data integration architectures to support both transactional and analytical systems.




Ensure that data architectures are designed to handle both structured and unstructured data, while considering performance, scalability, and security.




Cloud Data Platforms:






Design and implement data platforms ensuring seamless integration with existing on-premise or cloud environments.




Architect end-to-end data pipelines, including ingestion, storage, processing, and analytics, using modern cloud data technologies.




Collaborate with cloud engineering and DevOps teams to deploy, monitor, and optimize cloud-based data solutions.




Data Governance & Security:






Ensure that data governance practices are integrated into the architecture, covering data quality, metadata management, data lineage, and regulatory compliance.




Implement best practices for data security, ensuring that all data architectures comply with relevant data privacy laws and standards (GDPR, CCPA).




Work with security teams to embed data security measures within cloud platforms and on-premise systems, ensuring data encryption and secure access control.




Stakeholder Engagement & Collaboration:






Collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data scientists, and analysts to understand data requirements and deliver tailored solutions that enable data-driven decision-making.




Act as a trusted advisor to senior leadership, presenting architecture strategies, roadmaps, and solutions to solve complex business challenges.




Work cross-functionally with IT, cloud engineers, and development teams to ensure alignment on data architecture initiatives and their implementation.




Technology Leadership:






Evaluate and recommend emerging technologies, tools, and frameworks in the data architecture space, ensuring that the company remains at the cutting edge of data management and analytics.




Mentor and guide junior architects and data engineers, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.




Stay up to date of industry trends in data architecture, big data, cloud data platforms, and related technologies.




Qualifications:






Experience:






10+ years of experience in data architecture and implementation of enterprise data platforms.




Good experience working with hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP)




Proven track record of designing and implementing cloud-native data platforms, including data lakes, warehouses, and pipelines.






Technical Skills:






Strong proficiency in cloud data technologies (e.g., Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, Azure Synapse, Databricks).




Experience in data modeling, data warehousing, and data integration (ETL/ELT).




In-depth knowledge of data governance, data security, and compliance frameworks.




Proficiency in working with big data tools and relational databases (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL).






Soft Skills:






Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.




Excellent communication and leadership abilities, with the capacity to influence stakeholders and present complex ideas clearly.




Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative, and evolving environment.






Preferred Certifications:






AWS Certified Data Analytics – Specialty




Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate




Google Professional Data Engineer




Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP)

title: Data Engineering Intern - Graduate Program
description:
About the Role



   As a Data Engineering Intern at Thndr, you'll help power the data backbone behind one of the MENA region's fastest-growing investment platforms. You'll work alongside our data team to build, maintain, and improve the pipelines and infrastructure that turn raw market, product, and user data into reliable insights. This is a hands-on opportunity to learn how data engineering operates at scale in fintech, contribute to real production systems, and see your work directly support millions of first-time investors.








 What You'll Do






Build and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines that move data from a variety of sources (transactional databases, third-party APIs, market data feeds, event streams) into our data warehouse.




Help design and optimize data models and database schemas, applying solid reasoning about indexing, query performance, and trade-offs between different storage and access patterns.




Write efficient, well-structured code where choices around data structures and algorithmic complexity actually matter — many of our pipelines process large volumes of data where naïve solutions don't scale.




Reason about concurrent and parallel data processing — understanding how jobs run, where race conditions or bottlenecks can appear, and how to design pipelines that are correct under load.




Monitor data quality, investigate anomalies, and implement checks to catch issues before they reach downstream consumers.




Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, software engineers, and product managers to understand data needs and deliver solutions.




Contribute to internal tooling and documentation that help the team move faster.




Continuously learn — about fintech, investing, distributed data systems, and modern data engineering practices.






 What You'll Need






Currently pursuing or recently graduated with a degree in Computer Science or Engineering.




 Strong foundation in algorithms and data structures
 — comfortable reasoning about time and space complexity, choosing the right data structure for the problem, and writing correct, efficient code. You should be able to confidently work with arrays, hash maps, trees, graphs, heaps, and standard algorithms (sorting, searching, traversal).




 Solid database fundamentals
 — you understand how relational databases actually work under the hood, not just how to write SELECT statements. This includes indexing and query planning, joins and their cost, transactions and ACID properties, isolation levels, normalization, and the basic trade-offs between OLTP and OLAP systems.




 Working knowledge of concurrency concepts
 — you can explain the difference between concurrency and parallelism, recognize race conditions and deadlocks, and reason about shared state, locks, atomicity, and basic synchronization primitives. You don't need to have built a high-throughput system, but you should understand 
why
 concurrency is hard.




Working proficiency in Python (or a similar language) and SQL, plus comfort with Git.




Curiosity, attention to detail, and the ability to break down ambiguous problems into structured solutions.




Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively in a fast-moving team.






 Nice to have






 Exposure to distributed systems concepts
 — at least a conceptual grasp of why distributed systems are difficult and what trade-offs they involve. Familiarity with ideas like the CAP theorem, replication, partitioning/sharding, eventual consistency, leader election, or consensus is a strong plus, even at a textbook level.




Familiarity with modern data tools such as Airflow, dbt, Spark, Kafka, or cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).




Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).




Personal projects, coursework, or competitions involving data pipelines, analytics, or systems programming.




Genuine interest in financial markets, investing, or fintech.






 Who Are We?


Thndr was founded on a bold dream to democratize access to investing through smart tech and human-centric design. This is simply our way of saying we give anyone with a smartphone the simple and easy access they need to preserve and grow their wealth. At the same time, we’re shaping the future of investing while actively driving the economies we serve by promoting local investment products.


History has shown that investing is the single greatest way to build long-term wealth, but before Thndr, only a very small percentage of people had access to it due to:






High barriers to entry — In the form of excessive minimum account balances, complex, outdated onboarding, and low financial literacy.




Irrelevant experience — Catered toward expert traders and financial specialists, therefore alienating the majority of the population.




Fragmented offering — Investment products were not gathered in a single, intuitive outlet.




We don't just talk about change, we deliver it. Here's a glimpse into our impact so far:






 5.5 million
 app downloads




 EGP 1B+ average daily traded value
 across 2025




 #1 digital investing platform
 in Egypt for the 
third consecutive year




 76% of users are first-time investors




 40%
 of our users come from outside of capital cities and have previously had limited access to financial institutions




Building on the success of our core platform, we are continuing to change culture and break down barriers by launching Rumble, the subscription-based investment recommendations platform.


 Rumble 
was born with the vision to empower everyday individuals to build wealth confidently and intelligently through access to timely and in-depth advice from the industry’s top experts.


Going beyond traditional investment advice, it offers long and short-term investment recommendations and financial content through engaging articles and videos that guide users on their financial journey and maximize their returns.


At 
Thndr
, we're looking for people driven by our mission to help us democratize investing across the MENA region.

title: Software Engineer, Data Infrastructure & Acquisition - Alexandria, Egypt
description:
The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.


Over 50 million people use Speechify's text-to-speech products to turn whatever they're reading – PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites – into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify's text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its 2025 Design Award winner for Inclusivity.


Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting – Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.


 Overview


We're looking to hire for our Data side of our AI team at Speechify. This role is responsible for all aspects of data collection to support our model training operations. We are able to build high-quality datasets at petabyte-scale and low cost through a tight integration of infrastructure, engineering, and research work. We are looking for a skilled Software Engineer to join us.


 What You'll Do




Be scrappy to find new sources of audio data and bring it into our ingestion pipeline


Operate and extend the cloud infrastructure for our ingestion pipeline, currently running on GCP and managed with Terraform.


Collaborate closely with our Scientists to shift the cost/throughput/quality frontier, delivering richer data at bigger scale and lower cost to power our next-generation models.


Collaborate with others on the AI Team and Speechify Leadership to craft the AI Team's dataset roadmap to power Speechify's next-generation consumer and enterprise products.




An Ideal Candidate Should Have




BS/MS/PhD in Computer Science or a related field.


5+ years of industry experience in software development.


Proficiency with bash/Python scripting in Linux environments


Proficiency in Docker and Infrastructure-as-Code concepts and professional experience with at least one major Cloud Provider (we use GCP)


Experience with web crawlers, large-scale data processing workflows is a plus


Ability to handle multiple tasks and adapt to changing priorities.


Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.




What we offer




A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the company and product.


An entrepreneurial-minded team that supports risk, intuition, and hustle.


A hands-off management approach so you can focus and do your best work.


An opportunity to make a big impact in a transformative industry.


Competitive salaries, a friendly and laid-back atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture.


Opportunity to work on a life-changing product that millions of people use.


Build products that directly impact and support people with learning differences like dyslexia, ADD, low vision, concussions, autism, and more.


Work in one of the fastest-growing sectors of tech, the intersection of artificial intelligence and audio.




Think you're a good fit for this job?


Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply. 

   And don't forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.


 Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?

title: Microsoft Security Operations Analyst
description:

   Alexandria, Egypt (San Stefano)

title: SOC Analyst L2
description:
Job Description: 


Candidates in this role will respond to events according to documented procedures and industry best practices. Ideal candidates should be experienced in the areas of networking, client/server technologies, and analyzing log files with the ability to identify false positive and true positive events. Candidates must have experience in Linux and Windows operating systems. Candidates in this role may also be required to follow the incident response plan and assist SOC Response Analysts when necessary. Candidates must display enthusiasm and interest in Information Security.


Standard Job Requirements 




Provide advanced investigation of security incidents.


Conduct secondary triage and analysis on escalated events and initial remediation for escalated incidents.


Profile and trend events in the environment to determine if an incident needs to be created.


Provide communication and escalation throughout the incident per the corporate security incident response guidelines.


Communicate directly with data asset owners and business response plan owners during high severity incidents.


Communicate with Infosec teams on detected incidents that breached SLA/OLA.


Communicate with IT teams during incidents.


Hunt for suspicious anomalous activity based on data alerts or data outputs from various toolsets.


Advanced analysis of alerts.


Perform advanced analysis of log files.


Create, analyze and review reports and dashboards.


Perform advanced suspicions email analysis including mail header analysis, body and content or attachments.


Validate audit evidence (Ex: internal audit, group audit, PCI audit, etc).


Take an active part in the containment of incidents, even after they are escalated.


Escalate issues when necessary as per OLA and procedures.


Assist in continuous improvement of processes and work with IT teams to improve alerts and rules in the incident monitoring systems.


Review all Level 1 Analyst documentation.


Propose and enhance use cases.


Assign tasks to L1 analyst.


Performing administrative tasks per management request (ad-hoc reports / trainings).


2+ Years of hands-on SOC experience, covering the full spectrum of detection, analysis, investigation, alerting, reporting, and proposing remediation actions.

title: Credit Analyst
description:

Your role will involve analyzing financial data, conducting risk assessments, and providing insights to support informed lending decisions.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex financial data and make sound judgments.


Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with internal teams and communicate findings effectively.


Proficiency in using financial analysis software, Excel, and other relevant tools.

title: Part Time Junior Analyst — eCommerce & Market
description:
About the Role


We're looking for a Junior Analyst who can play a significant role in helping us better understand our customers, website experience and marketing campaigns, and help make data and insights available across the entire organization.


This is a high-visibility role where your work will directly inform marketing strategy, merchandising decisions, and customer acquisition investments. What You'll Do




Develop and maintain the key daily, weekly and monthly KPIs and reports


Analyze performance across key channels, such as Meta, Google, email and influencer / affiliate, and surface actionable insights for the marketing team


Help develop KPIs, reporting and insights regarding customer behavior, trends and loyalty


Monitor website performance including product-level trends, conversion rate drivers, and checkout funnel behavior


Identify tracking gaps, attribution inconsistencies, or data quality issues


Respond to ad hoc data requests from marketing, operations, and leadership

Help develop and maintain a library of standard prompts and AI-assisted workflows used for recurring analysis tasks


Evaluate new features and attribution models within Triple Whale and other platforms as they roll out, and make recommendations on adoption


Build agentic flows, orchestrations and agents to deliver quicker and deeper insights


Validate and verify outputs and results from AI agents and responses




Must-Haves




Fluency with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or similar) and a demonstrated ability to leverage AI to accelerate analytical work.
 This is non-negotiable. If you're not already using AI as a daily multiplier in your work, this role isn't the right fit.


1–3 years of experience in an analytical role, ideally within eCommerce, retail, or DTC marketing


Hands-on experience with retail analytics tools such as Google Analytics, Triple Whale, Northbeam or Domo, and comfort navigating multi-platform reporting


Experience with online retail platforms such as Shopify, BigCommerce or Salesforce Commerce Cloud

Nice-to-Haves

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist
description:
What Do We Do At Marketeers




































Data Analytics





            Our team is capable of collecting data from all around us and extracting the most beneficial data points and all the little gaps in data that get left unnoticed. Every day, they continuously learn new ways to look at data through training sessions that they learn and immediately apply to their work. Join a team that’s dedicated to developing their analytical skills every day for the sake of growth and transformation in this fast-changing market.
           










































Automation and R&D





            Our Automation and R&D team develop and optimize all our AI and statistical methods and models for our Data Analysis team to ensure that the efficiency and effectiveness of our tools always run as fast and as accurately as possible. Join an inventive team that’s always exploring new ways to innovate data tools.
           










































Market Analytics





            Our team has the statistical know-how and business understanding to analyze numbers and extract insights into actionable business strategies for our client base. Join a team of research-savvy business experts that guide clients toward their best opportunities for growth.
           










































Business Development





            Client engagement specialists are both research and business experts that have the understanding and skills to translate the needs of clients into implementable research directions and methodologies that the team guides from A to Z.
           










































Digital Marketing





            We support Marketeers by acting as their main communication tower by marketing all the company’s services and news to our entire network. Join a team that builds digital marketing strategies that are highly optimized according to everyday digital analysis that engages and grows our audience to ensure that Marketeers is positioned as industry leaders in our respective region and field.
           






































Operations





            We ensure high-quality data collection using different methodologies whether it’s online or offline for both qualitative and quantitative research. Join a team that works rigorously to monitor and maintain the whole entire data collection process by probing and navigating any issues that occur on the field all the way up till the project is complete.
           










































Accounting





            Accounting is a vital department dedicated to maintaining the financial health of Marketeers’. Beyond bookkeeping, we act as analysts and consultants that analyze research projects and overall expenses to recommend directions to ensure cost efficiency. This includes adjusting the operations team’s budget plan for projects, coordinating with global vendors and suppliers, and maintaining the company’s day-to-day expenses. Join an analytical team dedicated to supporting and diagnosing all of Marketeers’ financial aspects.
           










































People and Talent Development





            Marketeers works hard in headhunting the brightest minds in today’s market. We strive to find raw talent that matches each team’s dynamic based on personality and critical thinking to match our team dynamics. Join a team that aims to support the growth of all the company’s departments so that they can flourish into experts in their respective fields.

Manage day-to-day recruiting activities involving job postings, resume screening, interview scheduling, and candidate interviews.


Manage our relationship with universities and job fair enrollment.


Assist in employer branding strategy.


Supports HR communication projects and employee engagement events.

2-5 years of proven experience in relevant human resources positions, preferably in a recruiting agency.


Knowledge of recruitment tools: LinkedIn talent solutions, social media,…etc.


A pro-active, enthusiastic individual with the ability to operate in a positive, helpful, and productive manner.


Self-motivated and able to learn fast & grow.


Excellent organizational skills.


Strong communication skills.


Strong computer skills in MS Office suite: Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.








 If interested, please send your CV to hr@marketeersresearch.com with “Talent Acquisition Specialist” in the subject line.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
Who Are We❓


We Are Foodics! a leading restaurant management ecosystem and payment tech provider. Founded in 2014 with headquarter in Riyadh and offices across 5 countries, including UAE, Egypt, Jordan and Kuwait. We are currently serving customers and partners in over 35 different countries worldwide. Our innovative products have successfully processed over 6 billion (yes, billion with a B) orders so far! making Foodics one of the most rapidly evolving SaaS companies to ever emerge from the MENA region.




 The Job in a Nutshell

   We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join Foodics’ Optimization Team. This role focuses


on using data to improve product performance, customer experience, and business outcomes.


You will lead deep analytical investigations, design and analyze experiments and A/B tests, and


turn complex data into clear, actionable insights for product, operations, and leadership teams.



    What Will You Do❓




Own end-to-end analytical projects: define the problem, explore data, run analysis, and communicate results.


Design, execute, and analyze experiments and A/B tests to measure the impact of product and business changes.


Write advanced SQL to extract, transform, and analyze large datasets efficiently and accurately.


Perform deep dives to identify patterns, trends, and root causes behind business performance.


Build and maintain dashboards in Metabase and Looker Studio to monitor key metrics and experiments.


Partner closely with Product, Operations, and Business teams to define success metrics and track outcomes.


Translate analytical findings into clear presentations and written insights for stakeholders.


Measure the impact of initiatives and ensure learnings are documented and reusable.


Support data-driven decision-making across the Optimization Team.


Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data quality, define requirements for modeled tables, and request new data points or pipelines when needed.


Work closely with Product teams to evaluate product improvement ideas, build data- backed business cases, and prioritize initiatives based on impact.


Partner with Customer Support, Sales, and Operations teams to streamline processes, 

    remove inefficiencies, and optimize workflows using data.
 Navigate incomplete, inconsistent, or noisy data and still deliver reliable, decision-ready insights.
 


What Are We Looking For❓




5+ years of experience in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence roles.


Advanced proficiency in SQL, including complex joins, window functions, and performance optimization.


Strong experience in experimentation, A/B testing, and statistical analysis.


Proven ability to perform deep analytical investigations and uncover actionable insights.


Hands-on experience with BI tools such as Metabase and Looker Studio (or similar).


Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex analysis for non-technical audiences.


Experience packaging insights into presentations and clear written documentation.


Comfortable working with messy, imperfect, or partially missing data.


Highly analytical, curious, and detail-oriented mindset.


Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.


Experience in SaaS, FinTech, or data-driven product companies is a strong plus.







    Who Will Excel❓




Familiarity with Python and experience in data analysis using Python.
 Experience with datasets in the F&B or retail sectors.
 


What We Offer You❗
We believe you will love working at Foodics!








We have an inclusive and diverse culture that encourages innovation and flexibility in-offices.


We offer highly competitive compensation packages, including bonuses and the potential for shares.


We prioritize personal development and offer regular training and an annual learning stipend to tackle new challenges and grow your career in a hyper-growth environment.


Join a talented team of over 30 nationalities working in 14 countries, and gain valuable experience in an exciting industry.


We offer autonomy, mentoring, and challenging goals that create incredible opportunities for both you and the company.

Familiarity with workflow automation tools (Power Automate or equivalent).


Ability to translate operational requirements into structured system specifications.


Working understanding of reporting, KPI tracking, and data implications of process design.




Experience

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
Senior Data Analyst




Purpose of the Job


The Senior Data Analyst plays a critical role in leading advanced data analysis initiatives that uncover meaningful patterns and trends, supporting data-informed decision-making across the organization. This role involves independently sourcing, transforming, and interpreting diverse datasets and translating findings into clear, actionable insights through advanced visualizations and reports tailored for managers and functional leads. By strengthening our data-driven approach, the Senior Data Analyst will play a pivotal role in turning raw information into measurable improvements that support our mission and align with organizational objectives.





    Essential Functions and Responsibilities






Partner with directors and managers across departments to proactively identify opportunities where data analytics can enhance operational effectiveness and support business goals.




Lead the design, implementation, and deployment of predictive and prescriptive models.




Continuously evaluate, refine, and optimize models to improve accuracy, reliability, and business relevance.




Translate machine learning outputs into actionable recommendations that support data-informed decisions by directors and managers.




Deliver impactful reports, dashboards, and data stories tailored for executive audiences and non-technical stakeholders, clearly linking analytics to strategic recommendations.




Design and implement robust data collection strategies from multiple sources (enterprise databases, APIs, external data feeds, unstructured data) while ensuring data integrity and accessibility.




Lead data cleaning, transformation, and validation processes, establishing best practices for data quality, governance, and reliability.




Drive the development and deployment of predictive and prescriptive models to support strategic planning, resource allocation, and operational optimization.




Collaborate with data analysts, developers, and technology leaders to define data architecture, integration requirements, and system implementations.




Provide guidance to junior analysts and contribute to building data literacy across teams.




Proactively research and recommend emerging data technologies, tools, and practices to strengthen analytical capabilities and maintain competitive advantage.







    Required Education, Experience, Knowledge, and Skills






Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems Management or a related field.




3-5 years of relevant professional experience in data analysis, including designing dashboards, delivering reports, and supporting operational or business decisions.




Strong programming experience in Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) or R, with advanced SQL skills for complex queries and database management.




Demonstrated knowledge of ETL frameworks and data warehousing principles.




Strong knowledge of database design, relational and dimensional modeling, and data mining techniques.




Hands-on experience applying advanced statistical concepts, supervised/unsupervised learning, and machine learning algorithms to solve real-world business problems.




Proven ability to design, implement, and deploy predictive and prescriptive models.




Ability to evaluate model performance, optimize accuracy, and translate outputs into actionable business strategies.




Strong experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar).




Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) and associated analytics services.




Experience with web analytics platforms (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics) is a plus.




Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), as well as Outlook, Teams, and SharePoint.




Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with confidence in presenting to directors & managers.




Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for diverse audiences.




Collaborative mindset and willingness to share knowledge with team members.




Proficient in both written and spoken English.




Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills.




Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality work independently.







    Travel and Working Requirements 






Limited to a typical office environment, such as walking, sitting, typing, using office equipment, and occasional lifting of boxes or luggage during travel.




Occasional national or international travel may be required.




Working out of the official hours and on weekends may be required.

description:

 is the first B2B startup specialized in supporting social sellers. We handle product selection, storage, logistics, payment collection, and customer service on behalf of our merchants.




Launched in 2019 with a team of just 8 people, we’ve grown to over 350 employees across Egypt, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and more recently, Morocco. We serve over 
34,000 social commerce sellers
 from diverse backgrounds — from students seeking side income to seasoned digital marketers aiming to become independent entrepreneurs. Our sellers have access to over 
2,500 high-potential products
.


Our teams are driven by our mission and deeply motivated to provide the best experience for our sellers. With a commitment to quality and operational excellence, we're transforming the social commerce landscape in the MENA region!





    Our Mission


To empower anyone to start and grow their own e-commerce business.





    Our Vision


We envision a world where 
anyone
 can sell online, earn a living, and even build wealth — all within a simple, low-risk environment. A world where the magic of technology is made accessible to the most talented merchants.

You'll work in an international environment with team members from over 10 nationalities.


You’ll have access to a 
very attractive compensation plan
, depending on your ability to scale.


We invest in team development and prioritize 
internal promotions
.

ambitious, kind, and talented individuals.

title: Performance & Planning Lead
description:
About Taager


Taager is the first B2B startup focused on social sellers- Merchants. We are democratizing the social e-commerce space by enabling budding as well as seasoned entrepreneurs to sell online with no required capital, inventory, or operational experience. We have our Merchants covered from merchandising, warehousing, shipping, cash collection and customer service.






In 2019 we started as a team of 8 people, now we are over 350 employees across Egypt, KSA and the UAE. We serve more than 34K social e-commerce sellers from incredibly diverse backgrounds – from students earning a part-time income to highly ambitious digital marketing experts looking to become solo entrepreneurs. The sellers on our platform benefit from access to 2,500+ highly marketable products. Our people are driven by our mission and motivated by our desire to deliver the most seamless customer experience to the sellers on our platform. With a continuous focus on quality and execution, we are changing the social e-commerce landscape in the MENA region!






 Our Mission


Our mission is to empower anyone to start and scale their e-commerce business.






 Our Vision


We envision a world where everyone has the opportunity to sell online, make a living and even get rich in an easy and low-risk environment. A world where the magic of technology becomes accessible to the most talented of Merchants.






 Why Taager?


You'll be working within an organization with diverse teams based in different countries around the globe (10 different nationalities).


You will be welcomed to our vibrant fast-paced office in New Maadi, Cairo.


We pay fair market rates and offer a comprehensive compensation package (Salary + Stock Options + Performance based Salary Increases), as we believe our employees should be compensated fairly for their talent and capabilities.


We invest in our people's growth and promote internal succession.


You will be working alongside talented, caring, and ambitious individuals. We're very intentional about our selection process so that we hire people who can help us become a vibrant and healthy work environment for everyone.




️We offer a comprehensive medical insurance package.




Unique and profitable business model.





    Position Summary


As the Performance & Planning Lead at Taager, you will be the strategic architect of our data-driven decision-making framework. You will move beyond analysis to own the narrative of our business performance, translating complex data into actionable strategies that drive growth, optimize operations, and maximize profitability. This is a leadership role that requires a blend of deep analytical expertise, strategic vision, and the ability to influence cross-functional roadmaps.


 What will you do
:


 Strategic Leadership & Influence
:




Partner with senior leadership to define key performance indicators (KPIs) and establish a robust performance management framework across the organization.






Synthesize data from multiple sources to tell a compelling story about business performance, identifying not just what is happening, but why, and what we should do about it.






Champion a culture of data-driven accountability and continuous improvement.




 Advanced Analytics & Business Intelligence
:




Own the development and maintenance of scalable data models, dashboards, and reporting suites that provide a single source of truth for the business.






Conduct deep-dive analyses on financial performance, customer lifetime value, marketing ROI, and operational efficiency to uncover hidden opportunities and risks.






Lead complex ad-hoc strategic projects to address critical business questions, from market expansion to new product feasibility.




 Planning, Forecasting & Governance
:




Implement and manage a rolling forecast process to provide dynamic insights and enable proactive course correction.






Establish and govern the standards for data integrity, reporting, and financial analysis across the company.




 Team Development & Cross-Functional Collaboration
:




Mentor and develop a high-performing analyst (current or future), fostering their analytical and business acumen.






Act as a central hub for business intelligence, collaborating closely with Finance, Marketing, Sales, and Operations to align goals and drive company-wide strategic initiatives.




 What You Bring to the Role (Qualifications)




Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, or a related field.






4+ years of progressive experience in performance management, business intelligence, strategic finance, or a similar role, with a proven track record of leading strategic projects.






Demonstrated experience in mentoring junior analysts or leading cross-functional initiatives.






Expert-level proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools (e.g., SQL, Python/R, Tableau/Power BI/Looker); advanced financial modeling in Excel is a must.






Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distill complex concepts into clear, actionable insights for executive audiences.






Strategic, forward-thinking mindset with a strong bias for action and impact.




 Preferred Qualifications




Experience in a high-growth tech startup or e-commerce environment.






Deep knowledge of the Egyptian market and its unique business landscape.

title: Projects Lead, International Cooperation Focus, Project Management Unit
description:
Women play a key role in Egypt’s horticulture sector, particularly in processing firms and pack houses, and considering their priorities and constraints will be key to achieving the Project’s objectives. Similarly, farm-level participation in decisions regarding investment or new crop choices are often made by husbands and wives together, but the information that informs decisions is most often targeted at men. Led by Gender Integration Lead Ms. Ghada Hammam, the Project is integrating women and family units as agents of change, and give women more exposure to resources and information so that they can enhance their roles in all parts of the value chain. In any farm-level training we emphasize the level of participation decision-making, such as family budgeting for farm investment for both men and women with knowledge.










The Gender Analyst will be responsible for providing high quality technical assistance in ensuring the quality of the gender-responsiveness and mainstreaming of the project, as well as knowledge and capacity development services, to the beneficiary communities and target groups through conducting training programs to target population, both males and females, of fresh university graduates and women beneficiaries in target areas in Delta & Upper Egypt. The Analyst will work together with and under the supervision of Gender Integration Lead to fulfil all project requirements. The analyst will participate in delivering TOT trainings to university graduates and teachers of Agricultures Technical Schools (ATS) in target governorates to increase their knowledge about gender concepts, gender analysis, gender mainstreaming activities, and improve their skills in conducting gender awareness sessions. Trained university graduates and teachers of ATS will participate in the project activities, and they will have an important role in gender-sensitive social and behavioral change communication. The analyst will also support in the application of tools, methodologies and innovation for participatory and gender-responsive design, implementation and monitoring & evaluation of the project activities, updating the gender concept note as well as conducting relevant research.


 ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:


 Activities




Provide technical inputs to the Gender Integration Lead to support the planning and execution of gender activities.


Contribute to the mainstreaming gender equality into all project activities and on the level of the project’s staff and consultants.


Support the Gender Integration Lead in application of tools, methodologies and innovation for participatory and gender-responsive design, implementation and monitoring and evaluation of the project activities.


Support in updating gender concept notes and conduct relevant research.


Support the Gender Integration Lead in developing and implementing gender responsive data collection tools to track project progress and monitor indicators in the results framework.


Serve as a technical resource person incapacity building, knowledge sharing events and other relevant activities on gender equality and rural women’s empowerment.


Ensure knowledge sharing, documentation and dissemination of good practices on gender equality within the project and to the concerned stakeholders.


Collect and analyse quantitative and qualitative data using different research methods to develop gender related research and concept papers.


Facilitate gender initiatives supporting rural women in the project governorates.


Draft and review gender related Concept Notes, training proposals, ToRs, etc. and inputs to the development of RFPs.


Identify new methods and alternatives to disseminate the gender related knowledge to the beneficiary communities.


Prepare and update and test the gender component training manuals for the project’s different training models.


Participate in developing TOT training manuals.


Implement the gender component different training models.


Support Social Behavior Change and Communications Lead Expert in developing and delivering the necessary tools and strategy.




‍


 Deliverables




Research papers and documents promoting gender equality.


Support in developing training manuals/ TOT training manuals.


Draft reports on results of training activities and coaching sessions.







    ‍
Timeline


2 months (August& September 2024)


‍


 Required number of days breakdown


10 man-days per months


‍

Years of experience: 2 years of experience in developing gender related research, designing and implementing gender activities and training materials.


Education level and degree type: University Degree in Social Sciences, Business Administration, or any relevant degree is preferred.


Excellent knowledge of gender research, analysis, working with rural women and youth and local communities as well as with NGOs.


Excellent training and communication skills.


Readiness for frequent travel for project-related activities is required.


Previous experience in donor projects is preferred.

title: Senior Software Developer
description:
Nexera is looking for a Senior Software Developer to join our growing engineering team.




ABOUT NEXERA:


Nexera delivers mission-critical software solutions designed to help organizations streamline operations, improve visibility, and achieve measurable efficiency across their industries. As part of a global family of software companies, Nexera operates with a high degree of autonomy while leveraging shared expertise, technology, and innovation from a worldwide network.


Our focus is on creating scalable, intelligent systems that empower businesses to perform at their best. The Nexera culture is dynamic, entrepreneurial, and collaborative ideal for individuals who thrive on challenge, growth, and impact.




ABOUT VALSOFT CORP:


Established in Canada in 2015, Valsoft has grown to a global portfolio of 118+ companies, acquiring and developing vertical market software companies, enabling each business to deliver the best mission-critical solutions for customers in their respective industries. A key tenet of Valsoft’s philosophy is to invest in well-established businesses and foster an entrepreneurial environment that molds companies into leaders in their respective industries. Valsoft looks to buy, hold, and create value through long-term partnerships with existing management.




INVESTMENT APPROACH:


Unlike private equity and venture capital firms, we are Entrepreneurs who Buy, Enhance and Grow Software Businesses. That’s right; we don’t sell businesses. We form a strategic alliance with existing management teams. We recognize the dedication and perseverance required to create a firm and place a premium on customers’ and workers’ well-being over short-term goals.




CULTURE:


Valsoft is more than just a place to work; we’re a team. We mean it when we say people are our greatest assets, so investing in them is our number one priority. We create an


environment where our employees feel first-day-on-the-job excitement, day after day, creating a culture of high performers and collaboration. We celebrate our milestones, and we’re proud of them. We Dream Big, Stay Humble, and Stay Hungry.




POSITION DESCRIPTION:


We are seeking a Senior Software Developer to join Nexera’s engineering team in Egypt.


This role offers an exciting opportunity to design, develop, and maintain mission-critical software solutions that support global clients across various industries. As a Senior Developer, you will play a key role in the full software development lifecycle from concept and design through to testing, deployment, and ongoing support.


You’ll collaborate closely with product managers, architects, and other developers to ensure delivery of high-quality, scalable, and secure software. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced developer who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and wants to make a tangible impact.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable software solutions


Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to technical design decisions


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into technical deliverables


Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code following best practices


Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize application performance


Handle issues, bugs, inquiries, studies and other projects as needed by our analyst team and customers.


Ensure high standards of security, reliability, and maintainability


Stay up-to-date with emerging technologies and suggest improvements to existing systems






REQUIRED / MINIMUM QUALIFICATIO

   Requirements



   Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field




5+ years of professional software development experience


Strong proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, PHP, C#, .NET, Java


Experience with relational databases (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or MySQL)


Solid understanding of software architecture, design patterns, and development best practices


Familiarity with version control systems (Git or similar)


Experience working within an Agile/Scrum environment


Strong problem-solving and communication skills








Experience with AI driven development tools and techniques (CoPilot, Cursor, etc)


Knowledge of modern front-end frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue.js)


Experience with RESTful APIs and microservices architecture


Exposure to CI/CD pipelines and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)


Experience and enthusiasm for data pipeline development & ETL/ELT processes.



   Benefits





    Work in a global software company that values innovation and collaboration


Continuous learning and development opportunities


Exposure to modern technologies and enterprise-level projects


Hybrid or flexible working arrangements


Inclusive and supportive work culture with international collaboration






Ready to build impactful, high-performing software within a global network of technology innovators? Join Nexera today.

About Taager

   Taager is the first B2B startup specialized in supporting social sellers. We are democratizing the social e-commerce space by enabling entrepreneurs — whether beginners or experienced ones, to sell online without capital, inventory, or operational experience. We handle product selection, storage, logistics, payment collection, and customer service on behalf of our merchants.


Launched in 2019 with a team of just 8 people, we’ve grown to over 350 employees across Egypt, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and more recently Morocco. Today, we serve over 34,000 social commerce sellers, offering access to more than 2,500 high-potential products.


Our teams are driven by our mission and deeply motivated to provide the best experience for our sellers. With a commitment to quality and operational excellence, we’re transforming the social commerce landscape in the MENA region.


 Our Mission

   To empower anyone to start and grow their own e-commerce business.


 Our Vision

   We envision a world where anyone can sell online, earn a living, and even build wealth — all within a simple, low-risk environment. A world where the magic of technology is made accessible to the most talented merchants.


 Why Join Taager?




Work in an international environment with team members from over 10 nationalities.


Attractive compensation plan based on performance and scalability.


Strong focus on continuous learning, development, and internal promotions.


Join a team of ambitious, kind, and talented individuals.








 About the Role


We are looking for a strategic and analytical 
Electronics Buyer
 to join our Procurement team. In this role, you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of our electronics category, identifying emerging tech trends and sourcing reliable suppliers to negotiate the best pricing and ensuring inventory availability. You will play a critical role in ensuring our merchants have access to the most desirable and profitable electronic products.





    What will you do:








 Market Research & Trend Analysis:

    Conduct market research to identify new and trending electronic products (e.g., audio devices, wearables, smart home accessories, mobile accessories).


Source and evaluate potential suppliers and based on quality, price, reliability, and ethical standards.


Develop and maintain strong relationships with existing and new suppliers to ensure long-term strategic partnerships.


Negotiate competitive pricing, payment terms, delivery schedules, and return policies with suppliers. Secure the best possible cost of goods (COGS) while maintaining quality standards to maximize margins for Taager and its merchants.


Monitor supplier performance and ensure strict adherence to contractual agreements, including lead times, quality benchmarks, and order fulfillment rates. Address and resolve any supply chain disruptions or quality issues promptly.


Generate reports on supplier performance, pricing trends, and purchasing activity. Collaborate with data analysts to identify insights from purchasing data to inform future buying strategies and inventory planning.


Work closely with the warehousing and logistics teams to manage inventory levels and avoid stockouts or overstocking. Align purchasing strategies with sales and marketing plans for upcoming promotions.








 What will make you Successful:




Minimum of 
3 years
 of experience in a commercial buyer or procurement role, specifically within the 
e-commerce or retail
 sectors. Experience buying electronics or consumer goods is highly preferred.


Strong understanding of the consumer electronics market, supply chains, and pricing structures. Familiarity with importing goods and dealing with international suppliers is a plus.


Proven track record of successful supplier negotiations to drive cost savings.


Excellent communication and relationship-building skills to manage internal stakeholders and external partners.


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to interpret data and make informed purchasing decisions.


Proficient in 
Microsoft Office Suite
, with advanced skills in 
Excel
 for reporting and analysis. Experience with ERP or procurement software is a plus.


Proactive, results-oriented mindset with a passion for e-commerce.

title: Senior Business Analyst, Revenue & Operations
description:
About the role 
 
dopay is digitizing payroll for businesses across Egypt and helping workers access their earnings with dignity. As we scale, our Revenue and Operations teams need a single source of truth — one person who owns how we measure performance, where the data lives, and what it tells us to do next.


This is that role.


You'll be the analytical backbone for two of dopay's most strategic functions. You'll design the KPIs we run the business on, build the Looker dashboards that surface them, and own the semantic layer in LookML so that "active customer," "revenue," and "operational throughput" mean the same thing in every meeting. You'll work side-by-side with Revenue leaders to understand what's working in the funnel, and with Operations to find friction in onboarding, processing, and support. You'll partner closely with our Engineering and Data teams to ensure the BigQuery layer is clean, complete, and accessible.


This is not a role for someone who waits for tickets. We need someone who walks into a Revenue weekly, sees a number trending sideways, and shows up Monday with three hypotheses and the data to test them.
 What you'll own 
 


The KPI framework for Revenue and Operations. Define, document, and maintain the metrics that matter — from acquisition and activation through retention, expansion, and operational SLAs. Get cross-functional alignment on definitions and defend them.


The Looker semantic layer (LookML). Build and maintain explores, views, and derived tables so business users can self-serve without breaking things. Treat the model like production code.


Dashboards that drive action, not just inform. Every dashboard should answer a clear business question and surface what to do next. No vanity metrics. No 40-tile graveyards.


Deep-dive analysis. Funnel diagnostics, cohort behavior, pricing and discount effectiveness, operational bottleneck analysis, customer health scoring. Bring hypotheses, not just charts.


The data partnership with Engineering. Work with the tech team to ensure all relevant data flows into BigQuery cleanly and on time. Spec what's missing. Catch what's broken. Advocate for instrumentation when product decisions outrun our data.


Stakeholder enablement. Train Revenue and Ops leaders to use Looker confidently. Reduce ad-hoc requests by making self-service genuinely possible.


Forecasting and planning support. Partner on revenue forecasts, capacity planning, and goal-setting with grounded, defensible numbers.




 Requirements




 What we're looking for 
 
Experience:




4+ years in business analytics, analytics engineering, or revenue/operations analytics, ideally in fintech, B2B SaaS, or a high-growth operational business


Demonstrated ownership of a BI environment end-to-end — not just dashboard authoring, but data modeling and stakeholder partnership




Technical:




Expert SQL. You can write performant queries against BigQuery without thinking twice


Strong Looker experience required, including LookML development (explores, views, derived tables, persistent derived tables, access controls)


Comfort with BigQuery: partitioning, cost-aware querying, scheduled queries


Bonus: dbt, Python for analysis, Git-based workflows for LookML version control




Business judgment:




You understand revenue mechanics — funnels, cohorts, retention, unit economics — and can connect a metric to a decision


You're comfortable in operations conversations: SLAs, throughput, error rates, capacity


You ask "so what?" before you ask "how do I chart this?"




Communication:




You can explain a cohort retention curve to a non-technical commercial leader without losing them, and to an engineer without insulting them


You write clear documentation and you actually maintain it


Egyptian Arabic and English fluency strongly preferred for stakeholder work




Mindset:




Self-directed. You don't need a ticket queue to know what's important


Opinionated about data quality, naming conventions, and KPI hygiene — and diplomatic about defending those opinions


Energized by ambiguous problems in fast-moving environments


 What this role is not 
 
To save everyone time:




This is not a junior data analyst role. We need someone who has owned analytics for a business function before


This is not a pure data engineering role. You'll partner closely with Engineering, but you won't own pipelines, infrastructure, or warehouse architecture


This is not a data science role. There's room for predictive work over time, but the immediate need is descriptive and diagnostic excellence




 Benefits




 Why dopay 
 
We're building the financial infrastructure that lets every worker in Egypt get paid digitally, securely, and with dignity — and lets every employer run payroll without the friction that's defined the category for decades. We work with SME, mid-market, and enterprise clients and operate at a scale where the decisions you support will move real numbers for real people.


You'll work directly with senior leadership, see the impact of your analysis in the room, and help shape how a category-defining company runs itself.
